questions.question: What message is Malala trying to convey about the media? In your response, make a claim about whether the media helped her, injured her, or both. Support your claim with at least two pieces of evidence from the text, and explain how Malala's word choices help readers understand her view of the media.

responses.content: Both because because it says In paragraph 10 it says “ My mother used to tell me to hide my face when I spoke to the media because at my age I should be in purdah and she was afraid for my safety. But she never banned me from doing anything. It was a time of horror and fear” so if the Taliban were to see her she could be in danger! . But also it could help her to for getting her voice out there in  as much tv channels and radios promoting for girls to be back in school as she says they can’t stop her !
responses.bucket_rationale: The response includes a clear claim that the media both helped and put Malala in danger. It provides two pieces of evidence: (1) her mother was afraid for her safety when she spoke to the media, and (2) Malala used the media to spread her message on TV and radio. The explanation connects the evidence to the claim, showing how the media could be dangerous but also helpful. The student also explains how Malala's word choices (like "they can’t stop her") show her determination. The language and reasoning are appropriate for a fourth-grade level, and all parts of the prompt are addressed.
